message:
fix(journal,repair): correct JBD2 revoke-across-transactions semantics and fix log_add_exp edge case

ffs-journal:
Rewrite JBD2 journal replay to use a proper two-pass approach that
matches the on-disk specification. Previously, revoke records were only
applied within their own transaction, which is incorrect: a revoke in
transaction N must suppress writes from all earlier transactions
(sequence < N) to the same block. The new implementation first collects
all committed sequences and builds a map of (block -> max revoking
sequence), then replays writes in sequence order, skipping any write
whose sequence is less than the block's revoke sequence. This also
switches committed_sequences from Vec to BTreeSet during processing to
ensure ordering and deduplication, and fixes the incomplete_transactions
stat to exclude committed transactions from the pending count.

ffs-repair:
Fix log_add_exp to return NEG_INFINITY when both inputs are
NEG_INFINITY, instead of falling through to an arithmetic path that
produces NaN. This corrects beta-binomial tail probability calculations
when both log-space operands represent zero probability.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
